<!-- GENERATED by sphinxpress. Do not edit by hand. -->

<section id="outputs">
<h1>Outputs</h1>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> writes generated files for site and book workflows.</p>
<section id="jekyll-pages">
<h2>Jekyll pages</h2>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-site</span></code> writes one page per Sphinx JSON document under the configured <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">site.root</span></code> and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">site.tools_dir</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="navigation-data">
<h2>Navigation data</h2>
<p>For each project, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-site</span></code> writes a YAML file under <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">site.nav_data_dir</span></code>. Jekyll layouts can use this data to render project navigation.</p>
</section>
<section id="epub-and-pdf">
<h2>EPUB and PDF</h2>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-epub</span></code> and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-pdf</span></code> create a temporary aggregate Sphinx project under the configured work directory, then copy the final artifact to the configured output path.</p>
<p>PDF builds use Sphinx’s <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">latexpdf</span></code> builder and require LaTeX system packages outside Python.</p>
</section>
</section>
